changedFiles.reason: Declared the bounded implementation and verification plan.
Recorded implementation, verification, and residual release risks.
Generated lifecycle status for the active Work Item.
Aligned the static Contract example with ai-start required checks.
Aligned Summary verification and checkpoint examples with ai-start.
Added Python/platform and real minimal stack quality matrices.
Centralized default verification IDs and checkpoint stages.
Added read-only environment and configuration diagnostics.
Enforced static example parity and Agent Risk applicability.
Covered doctor failures and actionable warnings.
Verified ai-doctor script and Make target distribution.
Clarified application-language scope, review controls, and v0.4.4 adoption limits.
Applied equivalent natural Japanese positioning and release limits.
Applied equivalent Chinese positioning and release limits.
Removed permission-isolation implications from launch copy.
Documented runtime prerequisites, ai-doctor, and source adoption flow.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Examples must derive or assert parity against the same defaults used by ai-start.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Constants in ai_start drive generation and tests require both static examples to match.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Compatibility claims require executable CI jobs, not file-generation smoke tests.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Compatibility CI installs four presets into minimal projects and executes make quality.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Diagnostics must distinguish blocking prerequisites from configurable warnings.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: ai-doctor returns failure only for hard prerequisites and labels project customization as warnings.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Positioning must not imply filesystem permission isolation.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Landing and launch copy now describes independently enforced post-write review gates and names the sandbox limit.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Do not hand-edit generated cockpit status.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Status was generated by lifecycle commands.

risk.detail: CI coverage and public release state affect product claims, but source behavior is covered by tests and local stack execution.

observedIssues: The public v0.4.4 installer cannot generate first-adoption evidence.
The Python preset invokes python3 directly, so local verification required the virtual environment at the front of PATH.

residualRisks.detail: No new fixed public tag was published; v0.4.4 remains runtime-only for first adoption.
The Ubuntu/macOS Python matrix is defined but cannot be executed locally as GitHub-hosted CI.

reviewReadiness.reason: Source examples, diagnostics, positioning, and executable compatibility definitions are complete; publication remains external.

userCorrectionSolidification: Work remained on the existing main branch.

checkpointEvidence.detail: Scope, release boundary, example parity, diagnostics, CI, and wording reviewed before final edits.
Full quality, metadata, release contract, installer distribution, and four local stack quality runs reviewed.

knownGaps: A new public release containing source adoption support has not been published.
GitHub-hosted macOS and Python version matrix results require CI execution after push.
overclaimPrevention: Report local four-stack execution separately from GitHub-hosted platform matrix execution and do not describe v0.4.4 as adoption-capable.
